free_text: Neutron total cross section measurements on oxygen,
current 5A,pulse length 25ns, pulse rate 600 per sec.
by the electron beam bombarding on a water cooled
tantalum target, and were moderated by a moderator
6-Li glass detector (0.5 in. x 0.5 in. NE912).
The neutron detector was placed in the boron paraffin
Another 6-Li glass used as a flux monitor

From author: This compiled oxygen total cross section
is 3.85 barn at 0 keV which is about 0.1 barn (~3%)
higher than many observed values by others.
The difference could be due to moisture contamination
in Al2O3 powder used for neutron transmission
sample(1 mg of H2O in 1 g of Al2O3 may increase about
0.1 barn of oxygen cross section at low energy). To
get a reasonable value, I recommend to subtract
energy dependent hydrogen cross section multiplied by
a contamination factor (~0.005) from the compiled
SD: Comment from Dr. Makio Ohkubo added.
